title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins · Language Clinic: Index Routing
summary: Pages work from a local file but break below a project subpath, directories redirect unexpectedly, fragments point nowhere, and the team confuses filesystem paths with URLs. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the index routing increment in versioned URL and delivery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Define clean URLs, directory indexes, canonical output files, redirects, not-found behavior, and host-specific limits.
Model root, project-subpath, preview, custom-domain, trailing-slash, and relative-link behavior under one explicit base URL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-http-url-model-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-http-url-model-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_http_url_model_3(requests: int = 7, mapped_routes: int = 7, base_path: str = "/handbook/", broken_fragments: int = 0, media_types_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1:
        return False, mapped_routes, "request-shape"
    if mapped_routes != requests:
        return False, mapped_routes, "route-mapping"
    if not base_path.startswith("/") or not base_path.endswith("/"):
        return False, mapped_routes, "base-path"
    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Index Routing evidence · Theory Index Routing
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-index-routing-1sdmlt
# Competency: ssg-index-routing.
# Case 8530: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_index_routing_1sdmlt(requests: int = 7, mapped_routes: int = 7, base_path: str = "/handbook/", broken_fragments: int = 0, media_types_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1sdmlt = "1sdmlt-tlmds1-8530"
    assert evidence_variant_1sdmlt
    evidence_axes_1sdmlt = (
        "reader1sdmlt",
        "source8530",
        "parsetlmds1",
        "route1sdmlt8530",
        "artifact85301sdmlt",
        "recoverytlmds18530",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1sdmlt)) == 6
    if requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1:
        return False, mapped_routes, "request-shape"
    if mapped_routes != requests:
        return False, mapped_routes, "route-mapping"
    if not base_path.startswith("/") or not base_path.endswith("/"):
        return False, mapped_routes, "base-path"
    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"

Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
